content.full_text:
State of California - State and Consumer Services Agency
Pete Wilson, Governor

DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS
VETERANS HOME OF CALIFORNIA
P.O. Box 1200
Yountville, Napa County, California 94599-1297
Telephone: (707) 944-4716

February 26, 1991

Mark Morodomi
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

SUBJECT: REGARDING CONVERSATION OF 2-25-91

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

I would like to clarify what was discussed. On February 21, 1991, I called you to inquire into the recent legislation enacted on January 1, 1991, with respect to designated state employees receiving honorarium. My question was whether or not this new legislation applied to my receiving a speaker's fee for lectures given to health care professionals; physicians, nurses and pharmacists, in particular. I explained that the lectures are related to areas of medicine that I specialize in and also am actively doing research in. These speaking fees are paid to me by pharmaceutical companies that are sponsoring the given lecture. You told me that you would need to look into my question and would get back to me at a later date.

On February 25, 1991, you informed me that the nature of my lectures, within my field of medical practice and representing a standard part of my business function, and the receiving of a speaker's fee did not apply to the current legislation regarding honorarium received by a designated state employee. Therefore, my continuing to lecture and receive a speaker's fee, as described, is not limited by this recent legislation.

I am very appreciative of your looking into this matter for me and await your written response.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Steven L. Phillips, M.D.
Chief of Medicine

Veterans Home of California
P.O. Box 1200
Yountville, CA 94599-1297

SLP:jb
